message:
Add notification escalation ladder with persistence and desktop delivery

Implement a full notification escalation pipeline for the daemon, wiring the
existing EscalationManager into the daemon loop with persistence across
restarts and multi-channel delivery:

Escalation engine (decision/escalation.rs):
- PersistedEscalationState / PersistedTriggerState structs for serializable
  escalation state that survives daemon restarts
- EscalationManager::from_persisted() and persisted_state() for hydration/
  dehydration of trigger states and notification logs
- forget_key() to clear escalation state when a notification is acknowledged,
  preventing further escalation of resolved items
- has_key() for checking active escalation state
- Fix flush() to use let-else guard on max_severity instead of unwrap(),
  returning empty vec when no triggers need emission
- bundle_dedupe_key() using FNV-1a 64-bit hashing of sorted trigger keys
  for stable, dependency-free bundle deduplication
- Roundtrip test for persisted state serialization

Daemon integration (daemon/mod.rs + main.rs):
- DaemonNotificationsConfig with enabled/desktop/notify_cmd/notify_arg fields
- notification_ladder config field on DaemonConfig for escalation policy
- DaemonStateBundle gains notifications field for persistence
- Daemon loop creates EscalationManager from persisted state, re-creates
  on config reload while preserving escalation history
- daemon_refresh_inbox_notifications() scans inbox each tick, submitting
  triggers for unacknowledged items and forgetting acknowledged ones
- daemon_submit_inbox_item_trigger() maps InboxItemType to Severity and
  constructs EscalationTrigger with proper dedupe keys
- daemon_deliver_notification() dispatches to desktop and/or custom command
- daemon_notify_desktop() with Linux notify-send and macOS osascript support
- daemon_notify_cmd() passes notification metadata via environment variables
  (PT_NOTIFY_LEVEL, PT_NOTIFY_SEVERITY, PT_NOTIFY_TITLE, etc.)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
